fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 31-year-old White female with schizoaffective disorder presenting for medication follow-up.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived 10 minutes early for appointment, appeared cheerful and talkative. States she has been diligent about taking her aripiprazole 20 mg each morning with breakfast, tracking it in a journal she brought to show me. Reports feeling the best she has in years with no hallucinations for the past six weeks. Sleep quality is much improved at 7-8 hours nightly, and she wakes feeling refreshed. Appetite is good, enjoying cooking healthy meals, weight steady at 145 lbs. She recently started a peer support specialist training program which meets three days per week and is finding it very meaningful. Mood is elevated and positive, describing herself as 'really hopeful about the future' rated 9/10. She attends NA meetings twice weekly for continued sobriety - currently 14 months clean from methamphetamine. Lives independently in her own studio apartment and manages all self-care and financial responsibilities. Socially reconnecting with her younger sister after period of estrangement. Actively engaged in planning her future including potential return to community college. Explicitly denies suicidal or homicidal ideation. (Documented by Dr. Alan Friedman, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Neatly dressed in colorful clothing, well-groomed, vibrant appearance
Mental_Status_Examination.Speech: Animated, rapid at times but coherent, enthusiastic tone
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Process: Goal-directed, organized, somewhat tangential when excited

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC within normal limits; CMP unremarkable.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective Disorder, currently in remission, in recovery from substance use disorder.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives independently; enrolled in peer support specialist training; 14 months sober; reconnecting with family; strong recovery-oriented goals.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control on current aripiprazole dose.
Commended patient on sustained sobriety and engagement in recovery activities.
Supportive of peer specialist training as therapeutic and meaningful pursuit.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Alan Friedman, MD, Psychiatrist — Westside Behavioral Health, 923 Canyon Road, Los Angeles, CA 90049, Phone: (555) 203-4456, Fax: (555) 203-4457
